title: Proprioception, interoception, cognitive, and sexual-relational predictors and mediators for GPPPD
author: Mitra Molaeinezhad
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Using an embedded mixed-method design with both quantitative and qualitative data analyses, this project  will contribute to the analysis  of the mechanisms by which sensory, bodily, cognitive, and sexual-relational factors predict and mediate the occurrence of GPPPD overtime among a cohort of volunteer women in a new heterosexual relationship and their partners. Afterwards, to examine the response levels of those women who developed genitopelvic pain to adding a new treatment protocol during a single-blind RCT. With the combination and triangulation of the quantitative and qualitative data, this project is ultimately intended to create a multidimensional model that explains the contribution of these variables to sexual pain. Data includes  signed consent forms,   Pseudonymised   completed questionnaires in quantitative studies and   anonymised   audio recordings of qualitative phases.

With the combination and triangulation of the quantitative and qualitative data, this project is ultimately intended to create a multidimensional model that explains the contribution of these variables to sexual pain. Data includes  signed consent forms,   Pseudonymised   completed questionnaires in quantitative studies and   anonymised   audio recordings of qualitative phases.
